summary: RevDeck v0.1 TUI, navigation, analysis memory, Function Radar, and report export should share one ObjectRef/Relation evidence-chain model so analyst actions stay navigable, persistent, and exportable instead of becoming isolated page state.

RevDeck v0.1 should keep function/xref recovery confidence-scoped: symbol-backed functions first, heuristic fallback for stripped binaries, and external analyzer adapters later if full CFG/callgraph quality becomes mandatory.

RevDeck v0.1 should treat determinism as a product invariant: fixture binaries, stable object refs, score reason breakdowns, migrations, command execution, TUI state, and report exports all need deterministic workspace-level tests before feature completion.

RevDeck v0.1 should make SQLite objects/edges plus ObjectRef the stable core boundary: importers/analyzers write normalized objects, TUI/workspace/scoring/navigation/findings/export all consume the same graph, while external adapters remain fixture-backed contracts for MVP.
